perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise specifically provided in this Agreement, all amounts due by one Party to the other under this Agreement, less any permitted deductions, shall be paid in full within sixty (60) days after the fiscal month in which such amounts were received by the paying Party, or the receipt by the paying Party of an invoice therefore, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise specifically provided in this Agreement, all amounts due by one Party to the other under this Agreement, less any permitted deductions, shall be paid in full within thirty (30) days after the fiscal month in which such amounts were received by the paying Party, or the receipt by the paying Party of an invoice therefore, as applicable.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the payment term from 'sixty (60) days' to 'thirty (30) days'. This creates an inconsistency because it is now not clear what the payment due is, 30 or 60 days, after the fiscal month in which such amounts were received by the paying Party, or the receipt by the paying Party of an invoice therefore, as applicable. This contradicts with section 7.1(a) regarding 'Digital Screen Usage Fee'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Digital Screen Usage Fee. On or before sixty (60) days after the end of each month during the Term, NCM shall pay to Network Affiliate $30.00 per digitized screen (i.e., a screen connected to the Digital Content Network), provided Network Affiliate provides the required attendance and screen count information as such information time frames are established by NCM on a monthly basis.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment term from 60 days to 30 days. The contradicted text explicitly states a payment term of 60 days for the Digital Screen Usage Fee. Therefore, the changed text creates a direct contradiction regarding the payment timeline, as it is unclear whether the payment is due in 30 or 60 days, according to the sections provided. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, where different sections provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties contemplate that the Effective Date will be on or about __________________________.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties contemplate that the Effective Date will be on or about March 14, 2011.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction. By setting the Effective Date to March 14, 2011, an issue is raised on when the contract will expire as the end date is no longer aligned with the term length, as defined in Section 8.1. This affects the contract term and raises questions whether the end date is 5 years from March 14, 2011 or the date the service first began.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless earlier terminated as provided below, the term of this Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of five (5) years from the Effective Date (the "Initial Term") after which this Agreement may be extended on mutual agreement of the parties (a "Renewal Term," and together with the Initial Term, the "Term"). If either party wishes to extend the Initial Term it shall provide notice to the other not later than 180 days, nor sooner than 270 days, before the end of the Initial Term. The parties shall then engage in discussions regarding renewal for a period of 30 days. If no agreement is reached during that 30-day period, then neither party shall have any obligation to extend this Agreement beyond the Initial Term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text sets a specific Effective Date (March 14, 2011), while the original text left it blank. This creates an inconsistency with the Contradicted Text, which defines the agreement's term as five years from the Effective Date. By fixing the Effective Date, a potential conflict arises regarding the agreement's end date, as it could either be five years from the fixed date or from when services actually began, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines or obligations, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: Network Affiliate shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide NCM at least six (6) months advance written notice (or such lesser time for notice as may be practicable based upon the date of execution of the agreement for such disposition and the disposition date) of the sale or other disposition of a Theatre, the loss of any Theatre lease, or its desire to permanently discontinue delivery of the Service to a Theatre (collectively, a "Disposition").
perturbation.changed_text: Network Affiliate shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide NCM at least one (1) months advance written notice (or such lesser time for notice as may be practicable based upon the date of execution of the agreement for such disposition and the disposition date) of the sale or other disposition of a Theatre, the loss of any Theatre lease, or its desire to permanently discontinue delivery of the Service to a Theatre (collectively, a "Disposition").
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the notice period for disposition of a Theatre from 'six (6) months' to 'one (1) month'. This affects NCM's ability to remove NCM Property from the affected Theatre(s) as stated in the following sentence. Subject to the provisions of Section 2.3 with respect to transfer of title to NCM Equipment, at least thirty (30) days prior to any Disposition, NCM shall be permitted to enter the affected Theatre(s) and remove any NCM Property.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the provisions of Section 2.3 with respect to transfer of title to NCM Equipment, at least thirty (30) days prior to any Disposition, NCM shall be permitted to enter the affected Theatre(s) and remove any NCM Property. Except in connection with a Disposition, Network Affiliate shall not be permitted to permanently discontinue Service to any Theatre without the prior written consent of NCM, which consent will not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed. The costs of removal of NCM Equipment from any Theatre to which Service has been permanently discontinued shall be borne by Network Affiliate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the advance written notice for disposition of a Theatre from six months to one month. The contradicted text states that NCM shall be permitted to enter the affected Theatre(s) and remove any NCM Property at least thirty days prior to any Disposition. Since one month (30 days) is now the maximum notice period, this directly contradicts the provision that allows NCM at least 30 days to remove its property, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency within the contract because NCM may not have enough time to remove their property. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, as different sections of the contract now provide conflicting deadlines.
